per_question.reviewer_summary: The agent correctly selected fundamentals tools for an earnings question, and both calls succeeded. However, the chain is slightly inefficient: query_fundamentals and get_fundamentals_history overlap heavily for MSFT; a single get_fundamentals_history call with the target quarter would have been leaner. The agent then incorrectly concluded that Q4 FY2024 data was missing, despite the returned data containing revenue and EPS for that period (the grounding sample shows revenue 82.886B, EPS 4.27). The process failure is not in tool selection or ordering but in misreading the tool output. The single most impactful change: after receiving the fundamentals data, verify the period label in the returned record before concluding it is absent.

efficiency.feedback: Two calls to similar fundamentals tools for the same ticker is slightly redundant; one call (e.g. get_fundamentals_history with the right period) could have sufficed. Minor waste.

ordering.feedback: Only two calls, no data dependency between them (both fetch fundamentals for MSFT); order is irrelevant, so no ordering issue.

recovery.feedback: No failed or empty calls in the trace (both returned ok items=1); nothing to recover from, full marks.

routing.feedback: call 1 query_fundamentals and call 2 get_fundamentals_history are exactly the right tools for an earnings-metric question; no mismatch with intent.
